body:
@PathVariable vs @RequestParam:

• /book/{id} → path variable
• /book?title=foo → query param

Mixing them up is a classic mistake. Get it right once.

Raw exceptions in controllers are fine for demos.

Production? Map them with @ControllerAdvice + stable JSON.

Stop returning stack traces to clients.

Return structure: timestamp, status, error, message, path.

Pair with @ExceptionHandler + @ControllerAdvice.

RuntimeException("Database connection failed!") in a controller teaches one thing:

Always map exceptions to HTTP status + safe JSON body.

Production error JSON checklist:
✅ timestamp
✅ status
✅ error (short code)
✅ message (user-safe)
✅ path

Never leak SQL or internal hostnames.

Nested config + validation:

@Valid on nested objects is easy to forget.

timeout: 0 in YAML with @Min(1) → Bean Validation stops the app.

Better than discovering it in prod at 3 a.m.

JpaRepository<User, Long> — that's your entire DAO for CRUD.

Spring Data generates the implementation.
code:
public interface UserRepository
    extends JpaRepository<User, Long> {}

body:
Processing 1M rows with findAll() will OOM you.

Use a Stream instead.
code:
@Query("SELECT u FROM User u")
Stream<User> streamAllUsers();

body: Stream + EntityManager.clear() every N rows keeps the persistence context from growing forever.
@Transactional(readOnly = true) on read-only batch jobs — hints to the DB and avoids surprises.
Spring Kafka producer in 5 lines:
Kafka consumer side:

auto-offset-reset: earliest in application.yml — new consumer groups read from the start.

Know when you want latest instead.

Decouple services: save to DB, then publish.

OrderService saves → publishEvent(OrderCreatedEvent).

Listeners react without tight coupling.
Don't send the email before commit:
Don't use the default executor in prod. Define your pool:

Fire 10 async tasks at startup with CommandLineRunner.

Watch thread names in logs — you'll see Async-*.

@Async methods must be called from another Spring bean, not the same class.

Self-invocation = synchronous. Classic gotcha.
Slow external API? Cache it:
Redis cache TTL in one bean:

@EnableCaching + @Cacheable — Spring abstracts Redis.

Swap cache provider later without touching business logic.
Global CORS in Spring Boot — configure once, no @CrossOrigin on every controller.

Frontend on :3000, API on :8080?

CORS isn't a bug — it's the browser. Configure it once globally.
Debug HTTP issues with CommonsRequestLoggingFilter:
Spring Batch: read CSV → process → write in chunks of 5:
FlatFileItemReaderBuilder + .linesToSkip(1) — skip the header row automatically.
ItemProcessor transforms each row:
Parse CSV without reinventing the wheel — Apache Commons CSV + try-with-resources.

@MockBean stubs can leak between tests unless reset.

Use @DirtiesContext or Mockito reset.

@SpringBootTest loads the full context.

Use it when you need integration truth — not for every unit test.

@Sql on tests = reproducible DB state.

No flaky "works on my machine" seeds.
Builder pattern without Lombok — required fields in constructor, optional fields chain.
LinkedHashMap preserves insertion order. HashMap does not.

Parsing "121,234" as a double?

Quick fix: replace comma with dot.

Locale-aware? NumberFormat.getInstance(Locale.ITALY).
Italian number format 123.111,234 — don't assume . is always the decimal separator.

Shallow clone: super.clone() — shared nested objects.
Deep clone: clone nested Dependency too.

Cloneable without overriding clone() correctly → CloneNotSupportedException.

Prefer copy constructors or records for new code.

Spring Boot 2.7 + Java 17 in codingstrain — run any module with:

mvn spring-boot:run

Each folder is an independent lesson.

codingstrain.com

H2 in-memory DB for REST tutorials — zero Docker, full CRUD.

Swap to Postgres later; controllers stay the same.

/actuator/health before you deploy.

Monitoring isn't optional.
Spring Cloud Config (native + Git) in codingstrain — centralize config before you have 12 copies of application.properties.

Eureka service discovery — register clients, call by name not IP.

Microservices 101 in this monorepo.
OpenFeign + circuit breaker samples — resilience isn't an afterthought.

Contract testing (Pact) and Gatling load tests live beside the microservices.

CI-friendly patterns, not slide-deck theory.

codingstrain is a multi-module Maven lab — clone once, open one module, learn one concept.
